reforms.description: The UK government plans to increase public spending on R&D for low-carbon technologies, recognizing the need for higher funding levels to address the public good nature of basic research and ensure international coordination. This initiative aims to enhance technological innovation for achieving emission reduction targets.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D funding for low-carbon technologies is expected to foster innovation and contribute to environmental goals, which can enhance overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has proposed this increase in funding as part of its strategy for low-carbon technology development.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant increase in public funding for R&D, addressing a critical area of innovation and public good.
reforms.source_quote: The recent review of innovation policy by the Committee on Climate Change suggests that current government funding for clean energy R&D is at its minimum acceptable level and increases are warranted in certain areas.

reforms.description: The UK government has committed to spend up to £1 billion on a commercial-scale carbon capture and storage demonstration project and has established a Green Investment Bank (GIB) with an initial funding commitment of £1 billion to promote investment in green infrastructure. These initiatives aim to address market failures in financial intermediation and support the development of low-carbon technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in carbon capture and green infrastructure is expected to stimulate innovation in low-carbon technologies, contributing to economic growth and environmental sustainability.
reforms.status_evidence: Both initiatives have been officially launched and are in operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: These reforms represent a substantial commitment to sectoral R&D and funding for green technologies, which are critical for addressing climate change.
reforms.source_quote: The 2010 Spending Review announced the setting up of a Green Investment Bank (GIB), funded initially by a £1 billion commitment, to encourage investment in green infrastructure.

reforms.description: The UK government is changing higher education funding policy in England by increasing the cap on tuition fees to £9000 per year while reducing government grants to universities. This reform aims to ensure that universities have higher funding per graduate, which could enhance the quality of education and research output.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing funding per graduate, this reform is expected to improve educational quality and research output, contributing positively to the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The changes to the higher education funding policy have been enacted and are currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the higher education funding system, impacting the quality of education and research.
reforms.source_quote: The government is changing higher education funding policy in England, increasing the cap to £9000 per year, accompanied by changes to loans and grants to pay for tuition.

reforms.description: The UK government has set aside funding to extend the entitlement to fifteen hours per week of free early education to the 20% most disadvantaged two-year-olds in England by 2013, alongside introducing a pupil premium that provides additional funding to schools for each child entitled to free school meals. These initiatives aim to improve educational outcomes for disadvantaged children.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in early education is expected to enhance human capital development, leading to better educational outcomes and long-term economic benefits.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding initiatives have been proposed and are set to be implemented by 2013.
reforms.importance_rationale: These reforms represent significant but narrower changes aimed at improving educational access and outcomes for disadvantaged children.
reforms.source_quote: The government has said that it wants a stronger focus on supporting the most vulnerable and disadvantaged families and increased use of evidence-based interventions within Sure Start Children’s Centres.

reforms.description: The proposed Green Investment Bank aims to subsidize projects with a low social discount rate and will be allowed to borrow in debt markets to increase leverage. Additionally, the government has undertaken and published an evaluation of the UK’s R&D tax credit schemes to assess their efficiency over the longer term.
reforms.package_name: Green Investment Bank and R&D Tax Credit Evaluation
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of the Green Investment Bank and the evaluation of R&D tax credits are expected to enhance the effectiveness of innovation funding and support economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Green Investment Bank has been proposed, and the evaluation of R&D tax credits has been published.
reforms.importance_rationale: These initiatives represent significant steps towards improving innovation funding mechanisms but are not as transformative as major reforms.
reforms.source_quote: The proposed Green Investment Bank should be used to subsidise projects where a low social discount rate is appropriate.
